title: Workspace package robustness scrub 2026-03
question: What latent correctness, robustness, and cohesion issues remain across the Pi Loom packages after the recent path/artifact hygiene remediation, and how should they be fixed without eroding layer boundaries?
objective: Run a manager-led cross-package scrub that identifies latent bugs, weak robustness edges, and cohesion problems across all shipped Pi Loom packages, then drive durable execution for the fixes.
nonGoals: Invent new product scope unrelated to correctness, robustness, or cohesion
Re-open already-completed path portability and dashboard churn work unless new evidence shows regressions
methodology: Create durable execution artifacts for fixes and track package-cluster workstreams
Review each package entrypoint, tool surface, and core store/runtime logic for latent defects or cohesion drift
Run clean-workspace diagnostics and tests to establish current baseline
Use the prior hygiene audit as baseline context
statusSummary: Manager-led scrub completed. Ten concrete package issues were fixed across all shipped packages, validated with targeted regressions and final workspace verification.
conclusions: Dashboard and read surfaces must tolerate stale linked refs by surfacing discrepancies rather than throwing or under-reporting missing linked work.
Execution and review state machines must not let optimistic status transitions overrule unresolved blockers or newly introduced findings.
Metadata-only updates must preserve previously stored artifact content instead of rewriting durable truth with summaries.
Path-like durable references must normalize to canonical IDs before validation so artifact-path callers do not silently fail valid lookups.
Projection and orchestration layers must re-evaluate all fields they own when deciding whether existing durable artifacts remain current.
recommendations: Keep transition/waiting-state helpers authoritative wherever dependency or verifier blockers exist.
Prefer canonical-ID normalization at store boundaries for path-bearing inputs.
Treat dashboards/packets as defensive snapshots: clone exposed data and report stale links explicitly.
When projection logic reuses existing execution artifacts, compare every field the projection contract owns.
openQuestions: Do any remaining package surfaces outside the audited files still accept path-bearing refs without canonical normalization?
